page: Amazon Web Services m8idb.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors. The instance features 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores, with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring minimal virtualization overhead and dedicated resource allocation.

This instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the m8idb.8xlarge includes a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. This local storage configuration provides low-latency, high-throughput data access directly attached to the host hardware.

Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 33 Gbps. The inclusion of high-speed local NVMe storage alongside balanced compute and memory resources provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-speed temporary storage without the added cost of external network-attached block storage. However, because this is an instance store volume, data does not persist when the instance is stopped, representing a key architectural tradeoff.

The hardware profile of the m8idb.8xlarge makes it suitable for general-purpose workloads, relational databases, caching layers, and applications requiring high-speed local scratch space or temporary data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m8idb.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by dedicated 3.9 GHz Intel x86_64 processors. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware feature is the bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD local instance store, which delivers high-speed, low-latency storage directly on the host. The instance also provides a baseline network bandwidth of 33 Gbps. This combination of balanced compute, memory, and fast local storage offers qualitative cost advantages for workloads that benefit from high-speed scratch space, though the ephemeral nature of instance storage requires consideration. It is suited for database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose applications requiring local data processing.
